hypothesis: V28's exact target and support reconstruction are correct, but d128 at six epochs underfit the frozen V23 raw-logit function. Additional optimization may clear the trust gate at d128; if not, d256 may supply the missing approximation capacity while still meeting the measured A100 latency budget.
scope: Validation-only representation/optimization screen. It makes no strength claim and consumes no development or hidden evaluation seeds.

report: the 100 highest-KL row identifiers without private or hidden state
preTrainingDiagnostic.aliasDefinition: Hash exact float32 obsV2 bytes plus exact candidate/support bytes. If one identical student input maps to teacher distributions whose total-variation distance exceeds 0.0001, report the pair as an alias.
preTrainingDiagnostic.stopIf: A fail-closed schema/header mismatch or any exact-input teacher-target alias under aliasDefinition. In that case repair the encoder rather than spending more training compute.

result.conclusion: No exact-input encoder alias exists. Underfit is concentrated in strategic lockNavigation decisions, so V29 may test balanced strategic policy fidelity without repairing obs-v2.

selection.use: per-epoch metrics, early stopping, and best checkpoint selection

gate.use: evaluate exactly once after restoring the selection-half checkpoint

validationSplit.reason: Separating selection from the fidelity gate prevents choosing and certifying an epoch on the same games.

arms.optimizer: fresh Adam; optimizer state was not checkpointed
fresh Adam; optimizer state was not checkpointed
arms.purpose: Test whether shared-trunk auxiliary critic gradients, rather than width, limit policy fidelity.

arms.optimizer: fresh Adam; optimizer state was not checkpointed
arms.strategicKlDefinition: Scale equal-row minibatch estimates so strategic policy rows carry 50% of total teacher-KL objective mass and non-strategic policy rows carry 50%, each normalized by its full-dataset subgroup count.
arms.purpose: Diagnostic showed strategic rows are 25% of policy rows but carry roughly twice the aggregate mean KL. Give strategic and non-strategic rows equal teacher-KL objective mass without changing any target or support.
arms.select: minimum selection-half strategic teacher KL; early stopping uses the same metric

earlyStopping.metric: selection-half mean teacher KL for ordinary arms; selection-half strategic mean teacher KL for the strategic-balanced arm

sharedTraining.perEpochReport: training policy KL/top-1 plus selection-half mean/p95/p99/max KL, top-1, entropy delta, and strategic-only metrics
sharedTraining.freshOptimizerNote: The warm d128 arms begin above the V28 trust bound. A transient early regression under fresh Adam does not invalidate V29; only the restored checkpoint's untouched gate-half metrics decide fidelity.
sharedTraining.note: The arms are not FLOP-matched. This is a pragmatic fidelity/latency Pareto screen, not a causal claim that width alone caused a difference.

gates.teacherReproduction: The preflight teacher chosen-logp max absolute error remains <= 0.001.
gates.fidelity: On the untouched 128-game gate half: mean KL <= 0.05 nats, p99 KL <= 0.5 nats, top-1 agreement >= 0.88, and every metric finite.
gates.strategicFidelity: On gate-half strategic policy rows alone: mean KL <= 0.05 nats, p99 KL <= 0.5 nats, and top-1 agreement >= 0.88. Aggregate fidelity cannot hide poor terminal-credit states.
gates.latency: A selected checkpoint must pass A100 binary-wire p95 request latency <= 100 ms at 32 rows, 30 candidates, eight clients. The identical d256/l4/h8 architecture already measured 34.25 ms p95 in V27; remeasure the selected weights before the next stage.
gates.selection: Use selection-half metrics only. Among d128 arms that meet the selection-half fidelity thresholds, choose the lowest strategic selection-half mean KL provided aggregate selection-half mean KL is within 0.005 nats of the best eligible d128 aggregate KL; comparisons are paired per row. Ties prefer multitask, then unweighted policy-only, then strategic-balanced. Consider d256 only if no d128 arm is selection-eligible. Freeze this one checkpoint before opening the gate half.
gates.oneShotGate: Evaluate the 128-game gate half exactly once on the single checkpoint chosen from selection-half metrics. Gate metrics are pass/fail only and never rank arms. If the chosen checkpoint fails, stop V29; do not evaluate or fall through to a runner-up.
gates.d256Fallback: If d256 passes fidelity but fails the remeasured latency gate, reject it and stop; do not promote or silently relax latency.

selectionResult.decision: The plain d128 policy-only arm is selection-eligible and has both the best aggregate mean KL and the best strategic mean KL. The strategic-balanced arm is within 0.005 nats on aggregate but has worse strategic mean KL; multitask and d256 fail selection thresholds. Freeze the plain policy-only checkpoint and open the gate half only for this checkpoint.

losers.reason: Strategic p99 KL is 0.5340556174516703, above the 0.5 selection threshold.

nextStage: Freeze the selected checkpoint hash, then create a separately reviewed terminal-credit protocol using the still-unconsumed 944000000 development block. V29 itself never evaluates solo strength.

amendments.reason: The preregistered full-validation diagnostic found zero exact-input target aliases and localized fidelity error to strategic lockNavigation rows. Add a strategic-balanced policy-only arm before any V29 optimizer so the screen can distinguish raw compute, critic interference, capacity, and strategic loss allocation.

Correct selection leakage before training: rank arms and select epochs only on the selection half, match the strategic arm's early-stop metric to its purpose, and open the gate half exactly once for the frozen winner with no runner-up fallback.
V29 d128 arms launched only after both Fable reviews, the zero-alias diagnostic, selection-only trainer tests, and resource guards passed.

amendments.optimizerProgress: d128 arms reached epoch 6; d256 reached epoch 2; no checkpoint, stats artifact, exit code, or evaluation existed
amendments.externalEvidence: journalctl records causal-r18 Docker workers joining host networking on GPUs 0-3 at 01:54:46 UTC, and all four CARLA processes restarted at 01:54:46-47
amendments.reason: The SimForge worker surface reclaimed GPUs 0-3 and externally killed every co-resident learner. Preserve attempt-1 logs, then restart all arms from their original byte-identical init, seeds, row order, and hyperparameters after CARLA settled. This is not a model failure and does not consume selection or gate results.
